Name: Snivy
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It is very intelligent and calm. Being exposed to lots of sunlight makes its movements swifter.

Name: Tepig
Effects: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It blows fire through its nose. When it catches a cold, the fire becomes pitch-black smoke instead.

Name: Oshawott
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

Name: Snivy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

Name: Tepig
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It can deftly dodge its foe’s attacks while shooting fireballs from its nose. It roasts berries before it eats them.

Name: Oshawott
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: The scalchop on its stomach is made from the same elements as claws. It detaches the scalchop for use as a blade.

Name: Zoroark
Effects: Search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon’s attacks and use it as this attack.
Flavor-Text: Each has the ability to fool a large group of people simultaneously. They protect their lair with illusory scenery.

Name: Axew
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

Name: Ducklett
Flavor-Text: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

Name: Pansage
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are known to relieve stress.

Name: Pidove
Flavor-Text: Each follows its Trainer’s orders as best it can, but they sometimes fail to understand complicated commands.

Name: Axew
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They use their tusks to crush the berries they eat. Repeated regrowth makes their tusks strong and sharp.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

Name: Tropical Beach
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may draw cards until he or she has 7 cards in his or her hand. If he or she does, that player’s turn ends.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Pansage
Effects: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: It shares the leaf on its head with weary-looking Pokémon. These leaves are know to relieve stress.

Name: Zorua
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: To protect themselves from danger, they hide their true identities by transforming into people and Pokémon.

Name: Minccino
Flavor-Text: They greet one another by rubbing each other with their tails, which are always kept well groomed and clean.

Name: Darumaka
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: When its internal fire is burning, it cannot calm down and it runs around. When the fire diminishes, it falls asleep.

Name: Victory Cup
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Victory Cup
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Victory Cup
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Zoroark
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 20 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Bonds between these Pokémon are very strong. It protects the safety of its pack by tricking its opponents.

Name: Reshiram
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. It sends flames into the air from its tail, burning up everything around it.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

Name: Serperior
Ability-Effect: At any times between turns, heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Effects: Move as many [G] Energy attached to your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: They raise their heads to intimidate opponents but only give their all when fighting a powerful opponent.

Name: Emboar
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a [R] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can throw a fire punch by setting its fists on fire with its fiery chin. It cares deeply about its friends.

Name: Samurott
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Part of the armor on its anterior legs becomes a giant sword. Its cry alone is enough to intimidate most enemies.

Name: Scraggy
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It immediately headbutts anyone that makes eye contact with it. Its skull is massively thick.

Name: Axew
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: They mark their territory by leaving gashes in trees with their tusks. If a tusk breaks, a new one grows in quickly.

Name: Litwick
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Flavor-Text: Litwick shines a light that absorbs the life energy of people and Pokémon, which becomes the fuel that it burns.

Name: Victini
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, after you flip any coins for an attack, you may ignore all effects of those coin flips and begin flipping those coins again. You can’t use more than 1 Victory Star Ability each turn.
Effects: Move all Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon brings victory. It is said that Trainers with Victini always win, regardless of the type of encounter.

Name: Riolu
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions such as joy and rage in the form of waves.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

Name: Battle City
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may flip a coin. If heads, the player draws a card.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Reshiram-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kyurem-EX
Effects: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Hail Blizzard during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zekrom-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Thundurus
Effects: Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

Name: Tornadus
Effects: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.
Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

Name: Landorus
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s). (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as “The Guardian of the Fields.”

Name: Meowth
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It is nocturnal in nature. If it spots something shiny, its eyes glitter brightly.

Name: Volcarona
Ability-Effect: Put 4 damage counters instead of 2 on your opponent’s Burned Pokémon between turns.
Effects: You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: A sea of fire engulfs the surroundings of their battles, since they use their six wings to scatter their ember scales.

Name: Kyurem
Effects: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It generates powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

Name: Tropical Beach
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may draw cards until he or she has 7 cards in his or her hand. If he or she does, that player’s turn ends.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Mewtwo-EX
Effects: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Darkrai-EX
Ability-Effect: Each of your Pokémon that has any [D] Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Rayquaza-EX
Effects: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
Discard all basic [R] Energy or all basic [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of Energy you discarded.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Altaria
Ability-Effect: Your [N] Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: If it bonds with a person, it will gently envelop the friend with its soft wings, then hum.

Name: Lilligant
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Even veteran Trainers face a challenge in getting its beautiful flower to bloom. This Pokémon is popular with celebrities.

Name: Lillipup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It faces strong opponents with great courage. But, when at a disadvantage in a fight, this intelligent Pokémon flees.

Name: Keldeo
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Aqua Blade attack’s base damage is 120.
Flavor-Text: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.

Name: Flygon
Ability-Effect: At any time between turns, if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard a [G] Energy and a [F] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Known as “The Desert Spirit,” this Pokémon hides in the sandstorms it causes by beating its wings.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It occasionally uses an electric shock to recharge a fellow Pikachu that is in a weakened state.

Name: Elgyem
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has any [P] Energy attached to it, this Pokémon has no Weakness.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent’s brain, causing unendurable headaches.

Name: Empoleon
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.
You may discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If anyone were to hurt its pride, it would slash them with wings that can cleave through an ice floe.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Their sturdy tusks will stay sharp even if used to cut steel beams. These Pokémon are covered in hard armor.

Name: Crobat
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Flavor-Text: Having four wings allows it to fly more quickly and quietly so it can sneak up on prey without its noticing.

Name: Drifblim
Effects: Does 50 damage times the number of Special Energy cards in your opponent’s discard pile.
Put 4 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At dusk, swarms of them are carried aloft on winds. When noticed, they suddenly vanish.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: When it wavers its big, round eyes, it begins singing a lullaby that makes everyone drowsy.

Name: Ninetales
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 50 more damage for each Special Condition affecting the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its nine tails are said to be imbued with a mystic power. It can live for a thousand years.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
You may discard all [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: The tip of its tail shines brightly. In the olden days, people sent signals using the tail’s light.

Name: Virizion
Ability-Effect: Each of this Pokémon’s attacks does 50 more damage to [D] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon fought humans in order to protect its friends. Legends about it continue to be passed down.

Name: Terrakion
Ability-Effect: Each of this Pokémon’s attacks does 50 more damage to [D] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon came to the defense of Pokémon that had lost their homes in a war among humans.

Name: Cobalion
Ability-Effect: Each of this Pokémon’s attack does 50 more damage to [D] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: This legendary Pokémon battled against humans to protect Pokémon. Its personality is calm and composed.

Name: Keldeo-EX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 20 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Black Kyurem-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: White Kyurem-EX
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
Discard 2 [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

Name: Meloetta
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Many famous songs have been inspired by the melodies that Meloetta plays.

Name: Black Kyurem
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

Name: White Kyurem
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

Name: Metagross
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Team Plasma card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. You may not use an Ability with the same name during your turn.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: With four linked brains, it’s more intelligent than a supercomputer, and it uses calculations to analyze foes.

Name: Electrode
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
This Pokémon does 100 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.

Name: Landorus
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 30 more damage.
The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with the Defending Pokémon.
During your next turn, each of this Pokémon’s attacks does 30 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It runs through the narrow tunnels formed by Excadrill and Onix. It uses its sharp claws to catch prey.

Name: Darkrai
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: To protect itself, it afflicts those around it with nightmares. However, it means no harm.

Name: Giratina
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 50 more damage.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

Name: Champions Festival
Description: Once during each player’s turn, if that player has 6 Pokémon in play, he or she may heal 10 damage from each of his or her Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Porygon-Z
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a Plasma Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: Its programming was modified to enable work in alien dimensions. It did not work as planned.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may draw 2 cards.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: By reading the auras of all things, it can tell how others are feeling from over half a mile away.

Name: Genesect
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Overdrive Smash attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

Name: Thundurus-EX
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Team Plasma Pokémon.
If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Deoxys-EX
Ability-Effect: Your Team Plasma Pokémon’s attacks (excluding Deoxys-EX) do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If this Pokémon has any Plasma Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Lugia-EX
Ability-Effect: If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.
Effects: Discard a Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you can’t discard a Plasma Energy, this attack does nothing.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Genesect
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Discard all [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

Name: Tornadus-EX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flip a coin. If tails, discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.
ex-Rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Eevee
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: Mew
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may have your opponent reveal his or her hand.
Effects: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.
Flavor-Text: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

Name: Leafeon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It basically does not fight. With cells similar to those of plants, it can perform photosynthesis.

Name: Flareon
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
You may discard a [R] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Inhaled air is heated in the flame sac in its body to an intense fire over 3,000 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Vaporeon
Effects: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Recover 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its cell composition is similar to water molecules. As a result, it can melt away into water.

Name: Glaceon
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Move an Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: By controlling its body heat, it can freeze the atmosphere around it to make a diamond-dust flurry.

Name: Jolteon
Effects: You may discard a [L] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.
Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.
Flavor-Text: If agitated, it uses electricity to straighten out its fur and launch it in small bunches.

Name: Espeon
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of your opponent’s attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.
Effects: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.
Flavor-Text: Its fur is so sensitive, it can sense minute shifts in the air and predict the weather.

Name: Umbreon
Effects: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.
This Pokémon can’t use Slashing Strike during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: When exposed to the moon’s aura, the rings on its body glow faintly and it gains a mysterious power.

Name: Eevee
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: N
Description: Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a card for each of his or her remaining Prize cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Genesect
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.
Discard all [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.
